title: artemisia lactiflora var. incisa
intro: Artemisia lactiflora var. incisa, also known as incised white mugwort or Japanese sweet mugwort, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Artemisia lactiflora var. incisa include reducing inflammation, relieving menstrual cramps, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, menstrual problems, and stress.
